name: Tibidabo from Sant Cugat - circular through the Collserola Natural Park
name_original: Tibidabo desde Sant Cugat - circular por el Parque Natural de Collserola
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is characterized by its location within the Parc Natural de Collserola, offering a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region features extensive woodlands, making up over half of Sant Cugat's total area, and is traversed by several streams and rivers. Cyclists can navigate the Serra de Collserola uplands, experiencing varied terrain and a temperate Mediterranean climate. This environment supports a rich biological diversity, providing a scenic backdrop for touring cycling routes. Best touring cycling routes around Sant Cugat Del Vallès The most popular touring cycling route is Tibidabo from Sant Cugat - circular through the Collserola Natural Park, a 23.8 miles (38.3 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Turó d'en Corts viewpoint – Collserola Tower loop from Sant Cugat Centre, a difficult 22.7 miles (36.6 km) path. This route provides views from the Turó d'en Corts viewpoint and passes the Collserola Tower. Local touring cyclists also love the Chapel of Sant Medir – Sant Medir Hermitage loop from Mira-sol Centre, a 14.8 miles (23.8 km) trail leading through the natural landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is defined by its extensive woodlands, the Serra de Collserola uplands, and a network of streams. The region offers a wide variety of routes catering to different preferences and skill levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Cugat Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sant Cugat del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat del Vallès offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 1,120 cycle routes in total across different disciplines. Specifically for touring cycling, komoot features 47 routes, ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Sant Cugat del Vallès?
A: The terrain in Sant Cugat del Vallès is diverse, largely due to its location within the Parc Natural de Collserola. You can expect extensive woodlands, varied uplands of the Serra de Collserola, and routes that follow streams and rivers. The region's temperate Mediterranean climate supports rich biological diversity, providing a scenic backdrop for your rides. Options include road, gravel, mountain biking (MTB), and trekking routes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Sant Cugat del Vallès offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. There are 6 easy routes available, providing a gentler introduction to the area's beautiful landscapes. For example, the region research mentions routes like the 'Senderos de Collserola Con los Peques' (10 km) which are suitable for younger riders.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Sant Cugat del Vallès are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974468877/tibidabo-from-sant-cugat-circular-through-the-collserola-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tibidabo from Sant Cugat - circular through the Collserola Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 38.3 km loop. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó d'en Corts viewpoint – Collserola Tower loop from Sant Cugat Centre&lt;/a&gt;, offering great views.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Sant Cugat del Vallès is rich in natural landmarks and offers stunning viewpoints. You might encounter the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri&lt;/a&gt;, a 230-year-old natural monument. Routes also lead to panoramic vistas, such as from Puig Madrona, offering exceptional views of Vallès and the Collserola mountain range. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó d'en Corts viewpoint – Collserola Tower loop from Sant Cugat Centre&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint.

Q: Are there any specific regulations for cycling within the Collserola Natural Park?
A: Yes, when cycling within the Parc Natural de Collserola, it's important to follow specific regulations to ensure safety and preserve the environment. Cyclists are required to use designated cycle networks, give way to pedestrians, and observe a speed limit of 20 km/h. Cycling is prohibited on footpaths and single tracks less than 3 meters wide.

Q: How accessible are the touring cycling routes by public transport?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Sant Cugat del Vallès are conveniently accessible by public transport. Several routes are designed to end at Rodalies-Renfe or FGC train stations, providing flexible options for starting or finishing your tour and connecting with wider transport networks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Sant Cugat del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat del Vallès enjoys a temperate Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be warm, while winters are mild, though it's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region features several water bodies that can be incorporated into your cycling tours. Notable natural landmarks include the Els Alous lake and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Borrell Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4482632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallvidrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another significant lake in the area, offering scenic spots along its shores.

Q: What cultural attractions can I visit near the cycling routes?
A: Sant Cugat del Vallès offers a blend of outdoor activity and cultural heritage. You can combine your cycling with visits to the historic 9th-century Monastery of Sant Cugat, the modernist El Celler, or explore the Mercantic vintage market. The town itself is known for its green spaces, like Parc Central Sant Cugat, providing places for relaxation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Sant Cugat del Vallès?
A: The touring cycling routes around Sant Cugat del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of trails within the Collserola Natural Park, and the varied terrain that caters to different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes for those looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Yes, for cyclists seeking a moderate challenge, Sant Cugat del Vallès offers 17 moderate touring cycling routes. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32306165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Sant Medir – Sant Medir Hermitage loop from Mira-sol Centre&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.8 km trail that takes about 2 hours to complete through natural landscapes.

Q: Which routes offer views of the Collserola Tower?
A: For those interested in seeing the iconic Collserola Tower, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó d'en Corts viewpoint – Collserola Tower loop from Sant Cugat Centre&lt;/a&gt; is an ideal route. This difficult 36.6 km path not only provides views of the tower but also includes the Turó d'en Corts viewpoint for expansive panoramas.
tips:
The Pi d'en Xandri is one of the oldest pines in Catalonia and an ecological, cultural and historical symbol of Sant Cugat. It is estimated that he was born around the year 1774, at the time of Mozart, Kant and Napoleon Bonaparte, now about 248 years ago.

   It is a stone pine (pinus pinea) and is located in the Torre Negra rural park. It has a considerable height -23 meters- and a trunk of 3.20 meters in diameter. Its crown is 21 meters wide and has a peculiar shape, like a parasol, with a wayward, half-naked branch. The Pi d'en Xandri lost part of its branches due to the snow that accumulated on Christmas night in the year 62, when a formidable snowfall covered Catalonia in white. In 1997 some vandals tried to saw it off and set it on fire. It was necessary to graft a piece of live trunk so that the sap could circulate again and it was held with the trunks that surround it today to facilitate its healing.

The hermitage of San Medín is a sanctuary located in the Collserola mountain range, in the municipality of San Cugat del Vallés (Catalonia). Every March 3, the traditional meeting of San Medin is held there.[1] It is near the old Roman road from Egara (Tarrasa) to Barcino (Barcelona), which passing through Castrum Octavianum (Sant Cugat) entered the Collserola mountain range. through the Gausac valley (or Sant Medir valley).
Formerly called San Emeterio, the hermitage is of Romanesque origin, although due to the transformations it has undergone there are hardly any original traces left. It has a rectangular floor plan with a sacristy. Outside there is a bell tower and the entrance door.

Mythical ascent to the Tibidabo amusement park located at the top of the city. It has different slopes to reach it and there is the possibility of doing several loops to stretch the route more or less. There are very good views from the top and it does not take a great hardness to reach the top. Totally recommended!

In this area you have a romantic chapel, a restaurant and a picknick area.
The chapel is only open on Saturdays from 11 to 13 o'clock. The restaurant and picknick area are perfect for a stop when hiking through the Collserola park. The restaurant might seem a bit expensive, but for being in the middle of the park it's okay.
La Pedrera dels Ocells in the municipality of Barcelona is a viewpoint located in the Collserola mountain range near the Puig d'Olorda and the hermitage of Santa Creu d'Olorda.
